title: Upwaltham Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: All 8 killed (including Dambuster veteran John Pulford)
memorial_type: Memorial stone (Lancaster flyover conducted at dedication)

external_narratives.summary: Detailed crew page for Lancaster DV382 (KC-J) of 617 Squadron. The aircraft took off at 08:20 on 12 February 1944 from RAF Ford, where it had landed after an operation against the Antheor Viaduct in southern France. Within five minutes, in low cloud, the Lancaster flew into trees at the top of Littleton Down.
external_narratives.key_details: Crew: S/L William R. Suggitt DFC (Pilot, died of injuries 15 Feb at St Richard's Hospital Chichester), F/S John Pulford DFM (Flight Engineer, Dambuster veteran who flew with W/C Gibson on the Dams Raid May 1943), F/O J.I. Gordon DFC (Navigator), F/O N.J. Davidson (Bomb Aimer), F/O S.G. Hall (WOp/AG), F/S J.P. Riches (Mid Upper AG), F/O J.M. Dempster DFM (Rear AG), S/L T.W. Lloyd DSO (Passenger).

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the Lancaster DV382 crash memorial at Upwaltham, Sussex. Catalogued as memorial reference 106342.
external_narratives.key_details: Formally registered memorial in the IWM national war memorials database. Memorial stone at the crash site on Littleton Down.

external_narratives.summary: Account of the memorial dedication at Upwaltham where a Lancaster from the Battle of Britain Memorial Flight performed a flyover. The tiny hamlet of 25 inhabitants raised over 10,500 pounds for the memorial stone.
external_narratives.key_details: The hamlet of Upwaltham (population 25) fundraised for the memorial. BBMF Lancaster flyover conducted at the dedication ceremony. F/S Pulford's DFM was gazetted 28 May 1943 for his role in the Dams Raid.

external_narratives.summary: International war sites database entry for the Lancaster DV382 crash site at Upwaltham, documenting the 617 Squadron loss and the memorial location on the South Downs.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft code KC-J. The crash occurred while departing RAF Ford after an operational landing. S/L Lloyd DSO was a passenger, bringing the total aboard to eight.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
